objective: Quantify when square cavity displacements and Gaussian or DRAG qubit rotations in a dispersive transmon-cavity system still realize their ideal gate-level descriptions, and identify the regime boundaries where waveform-level corrections become necessary.
hypotheses: Square cavity displacements remain effectively unconditional only when |chi| T / 2pi is small enough that the excited-state cavity detuning stays perturbative.
Gaussian or DRAG qubit rotations calibrated in the vacuum manifold become Fock-dependent once n chi T is no longer small, limiting broadband spectator behavior.
A naive echoed displacement sequence will fail to recover unconditional superposition-state fidelity once the inserted pi pulses themselves become Fock-dependent in the populated cavity.
assumptions: Closed-system dispersive dynamics with no Lindblad decoherence channels.
Three transmon levels and a truncated cavity Fock basis are sufficient after the reported convergence checks.
The dispersive Hamiltonian with chi, chi-prime, and Kerr captures the relevant waveform-level distortions at the chosen operating point.
The echoed-displacement follow-up uses vacuum-calibrated Gaussian or DRAG pi pulses and therefore probes the simplest refocusing construction rather than a manifold-adaptive design.

success_criteria.displacement: Map fidelity, entanglement, and dominant-error trends for square cavity displacements over amplitude and duration.
success_criteria.rotation: Extract Fock-resolved effective qubit-rotation metrics and identify the spectator-safe cavity occupations and pulse durations.
success_criteria.validation: Pass sanity, convergence, and literature-comparison gates with explicit numerical evidence.
success_criteria.reporting: Produce a reproducible report, artifact set, and notebook that include the echoed-displacement negative result in the paper itself.

completed_tasks: Initialized the study folder, README, IMPROVEMENTS log, and shared helper modules.
Audited cqed_sim conventions for tensor ordering, Hamiltonian terms, and carrier definitions.
Ran the square-displacement study and saved fidelity, ablation, and phase-space artifacts and figures.
Ran the qubit-rotation study and saved Fock-resolved, DRAG, entanglement, and regime-map artifacts and figures.
Completed cross-regime synthesis and validation with explicit convergence and sanity-check evidence.
Wrote and built the report, bibliography, and reproducibility notebook.
Executed the echoed-displacement extension and saved echo comparison artifacts and figures.
Integrated the echoed-displacement evidence into the report body and appendix, then rebuilt the PDF successfully with pdflatex and bibtex.
Completed the approval-style review artifact and final polish closeout for the study.
Extended the report and study documentation to interpret the two-tone cavity pulse as the minimal multiplex drive and to record when richer multitone compensation is likely justified.

Ran an explicit multiplex-drive follow-up benchmark, archived the new artifact and figure pair, and updated the report around the resulting negative multicarrier result and the corrected broad-state ranking.
Ran segmented and jointly optimized structured multiplex follow-ups, updated the reproducibility notebook, and reconciled the archival report and state files with the final protocol ranking.

displacement.ground_state: For |g>, square displacements remain above 0.999 fidelity across the tested amplitudes for pulse durations up to 100 ns.
displacement.excited_state: For |e>, fidelity collapses once |chi| T / 2pi becomes appreciable; at T = 100 ns and |alpha| = 2.0 the fidelity is about 0.06, and at 200 ns it is about 0.002.
displacement.dominant_error: The dispersive shift chi accounts for more than 99 percent of the displacement error, while chi-prime and Kerr remain minor corrections at the studied operating point.
displacement.multiplex_interpretation: The successful two-tone displacement should be read as the minimal two-channel multiplex solution to the g/e branch split; the extended multiplex follow-up shows that extra carriers alone, segmented branch-resonant fits, and low-parameter jointly optimized shaped-two-tone refinements do not replace short branch-calibrated control or richer within-window temporal shaping.
displacement.best_broad_state_protocol: On the explicit 14-state benchmark evaluated in the multiplex follow-up, the 20 ns two-tone pulse is the strongest tested protocol, with mean fidelity 0.9857 and minimum fidelity 0.9242.

multiplex_followup.best_case: The best full-duration Fourier-compressed multiplex drive used 8 tones at 40 ns but reached only mean fidelity 0.5242 and minimum fidelity 0.00189.
multiplex_followup.best_segmented_case: The best segmented branch-resonant fit used 8 segments at 40 ns but reached only mean fidelity 0.3612 and minimum fidelity 0.00814.
multiplex_followup.best_joint_shaped_case: The best jointly optimized shaped-two-tone case used 4 segments at 40 ns and reached only mean fidelity 0.1576 and minimum fidelity 0.0459 after a successful local Powell optimization.
multiplex_followup.two_tone_references: The calibrated two-tone references at 20 ns and 40 ns reached mean fidelities 0.9857 and 0.9528, respectively, on the same broad state set.
multiplex_followup.interpretation: The negative multiplex results indicate that the sampled optimal waveform's success depends on richer within-window temporal structure, not just on the presence of additional carrier frequencies or low-dimensional structured segmentation.

qubit_rotation.vacuum: An X_pi pulse at 40 ns reaches 0.998759 fidelity without DRAG and 0.999841 with the optimal 0.5 ns DRAG coefficient in the n = 0 manifold.
qubit_rotation.fock_dependence: At 40 ns the Fock-resolved X_pi fidelity falls from about 0.999 at n = 0 to about 0.483 at n = 5, showing strong photon-number selectivity.
qubit_rotation.entanglement: Applying an X_pi pulse with the cavity in |alpha| = 2.0 generates about 0.54 bits of qubit-cavity entanglement and reduces state fidelity to about 0.36.

echoed_displacement.superposition_failure: For the representative fixed-displacement, Gaussian-20-ns echo protocol, the echo-to-bare infidelity ratio stays above 1 throughout the tested (|alpha|, T) grid for the |+x> input.

compute_notes.main_study: The main waveform and regime-map sweeps completed within the planned single-machine CPU budget and did not require GPU acceleration.
compute_notes.echo_extension: The echoed-displacement extension added a modest post-report parameter sweep over alpha, duration, pi-pulse family, and fairness convention.
compute_notes.report_build: latexmk was unavailable because the local MiKTeX installation lacks perl, so the final PDF was built with explicit pdflatex and bibtex passes instead.
compute_notes.multiplex_extension: The first multiplex-drive extension was documentation only and synthesized the existing two-tone, broad-state, and constrained-waveform results.
compute_notes.multiplex_followup: The extended multiplex follow-up ran in about 39.0 s and evaluated full-duration K = 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8 carrier approximations, segmented branch-resonant fits with 1 / 2 / 4 / 8 windows, and a successfully converged 4-segment jointly optimized shaped-two-tone family together with refreshed 20 ns / 40 ns / 80 ns two-tone references.
